summary: Tested Space and Enter key functionality in admin panel on desktop. WYSIWYG editors (article content, PDF generator) work correctly. Due Diligence textarea has a BUG: Enter key does not create new lines because the onChange handler strips empty lines.

ui_bugs.issue: Enter key does not create new lines - newlines are stripped by onChange handler
ui_bugs.root_cause: The onChange handler filters out empty lines with `lines.filter(l => l.trim())` and removes trailing newlines with `.replace(/\n$/, '')`. When user presses Enter, the empty line is filtered out, causing the cursor to jump back.

action_items: Fix Due Diligence textarea onChange handler to preserve newlines while typing. The current implementation strips empty lines immediately, preventing users from pressing Enter to create new checklist items. Suggested fix: Only filter empty lines when converting to HTML for storage, but preserve the raw textarea value with newlines for display.
critical_code_review_comments: Due Diligence textarea onChange (line 3001-3008): The `lines.filter(l => l.trim())` removes empty lines immediately, which breaks Enter key functionality. The value prop also removes trailing newlines with `.replace(/\n$/, '')` (line 2998).
WYSIWYG Editor handleKeyDown (line 566-578): Correctly lets browser handle Enter natively - no e.preventDefault() called.
Form onKeyDown (line 2711-2712): Correctly prevents Enter from submitting form only for INPUT elements, not TEXTAREA.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Due Diligence textarea Enter key bug confirmed. The issue is in AdminPage.jsx lines 3001-3008. The onChange handler immediately filters empty lines, preventing Enter from creating new lines. All other Space/Enter key tests pass: WYSIWYG editors, Expert Tip textarea, PDF Generator, and form submission prevention.

tests_performed.wysiwyg_editor_space: PASS - 'Hello World Test' typed correctly with spaces
tests_performed.due_diligence_title_space: PASS - 'Test Title With Spaces' typed correctly
tests_performed.due_diligence_textarea_space: PASS - 'First item with spaces' typed correctly
tests_performed.due_diligence_textarea_enter: FAIL - Newlines stripped, text concatenated on same line
tests_performed.pdf_generator_wysiwyg_space: PASS - 'PDF content with spaces' typed correctly
tests_performed.form_input_enter_no_submit: PASS - Form stays on edit page

rca_of_the_issue.bug: Due Diligence textarea Enter key not creating new lines
reproduction_steps: 2. Click 'Neuer Artikel' to create new article
3. Scroll down to 'Due Diligence Box' section
7. Observe: Text shows 'First itemSecond item' on same line instead of two lines
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The textarea's onChange handler (line 3001-3008) immediately processes the input: splits by newline, filters out empty lines with `lines.filter(l => l.trim())`, then converts to HTML. When Enter is pressed, the value becomes 'First item\n', which splits to ['First item', ''], filters to ['First item'], and the empty line is lost. Additionally, the value prop removes trailing newlines with `.replace(/\n$/, '')` (line 2998).
rca_of_the_issue.suggested_fix: Option 1: Use uncontrolled textarea with onBlur to convert to HTML only when user leaves the field. Option 2: Track raw text separately from HTML content, only convert on save. Option 3: Preserve trailing newlines in the value computation and only filter empty lines when generating final HTML.
